marketFocus: Trump gave Iran a 48-hour ultimatum to open Hormuz or face strikes on power plants. Iran's Revolutionary Guards responded: full Hormuz closure if energy facilities are hit. US preparing potential ground invasion of Kharg Island; thousands of troops deploying to the Middle East.

WTI settled $98.23/bbl, Brent $112.19/bbl — Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields; Basra production collapsed from 3.3mln to 900k bpd. Russell 2000 entered correction territory (-10% from peaks). Gold down 3%+ below $4,400/oz.

Asia-Pac stocks opening sharply lower. PMT: 'USD gains amid risk-off mood and bets for an October Fed rate hike.' CAD outperforms on oil surge. JPY bid on haven demand and carry unwind. New hawkish BOJ board members (Asada and Sato) confirmed.

USD.tone: Risk-Off Bid + October Fed Hike Bets — DXY Firming
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today on a combination of risk-off safe-haven demand and a fresh repricing of Fed expectations — PMT headlines explicitly note 'USD gains amid the risk-off mood and bets for an October Fed rate hike.' The 48-hour Iran ultimatum from Trump and the prospect of a US ground invasion of Kharg Island have driven a flight to the dollar. DXY is firming from the 99.99-100.31 range seen last week. Treasury futures remain pressured on oil-related inflationary concerns, which supports the rate hike narrative. Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: PMT: 'USD gains amid risk-off mood and bets for an October Fed rate hike'
Trump 48-hour Iran ultimatum — safe-haven demand surging
Treasury futures pressured on oil-related inflationary concerns
DXY firming from 99.99-100.31 range; October Fed hike bets repriced
US preparing potential ground invasion of Kharg Island — geopolitical premium elevated

EUR.tone: Energy Shock Re-Escalation Overwhelms ECB Hawkish Pivot
EUR.summary: EUR is Bearish today as the re-escalation of the Iran conflict — Brent $112.19/bbl, Iraq force majeure, Basra production collapse — overwhelms the ECB hawkish pivot story from last week. ECB's Makhlouf and Nagel both warned of second-round inflation risks on Friday, but EUR is an energy importer and the renewed oil surge acts as a direct tax on the Eurozone economy. ECB's Nagel: 'the higher inflation goes and the longer expectations remain above target, the greater the second-round risks become.' EUR/USD faces renewed downside pressure. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Brent $112.19/bbl — energy import shock re-escalating; Eurozone directly exposed
Iraq force majeure: Basra production collapsed from 3.3mln to 900k bpd
ECB's Nagel: second-round inflation risks growing; 'wait-and-see approach'
ECB hawkish pivot story from last week now competing with renewed energy shock
EUR/USD faces renewed downside pressure as risk-off dominates

GBP.tone: Geopolitical Risk Premium — Iran Missiles at Diego Garcia, UK Bases Used for US Ops
GBP.summary: GBP is Bearish today as the UK faces direct geopolitical risk: Iran fired ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia (UK/US military base) on Friday night, and UK ministers confirmed a deal for the US to use UK bases for defensive operations against Iran. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi warned that 'UK PM Starmer is putting British lives in danger.' UK PM Starmer is convening an emergency meeting of senior officials and the BOE Governor on the cost of living this week. The BOE hawkish pivot story is now overshadowed by the UK's direct involvement in the conflict. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Iran fired ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia (UK/US base) — UK directly in conflict
UK ministers confirmed deal for US to use UK bases for operations against Iran
Iran's Araghchi: 'Starmer is putting British lives in danger'
UK PM Starmer emergency meeting on cost of living — BOE Governor attending
BOE hawkish pivot overshadowed by UK geopolitical risk premium

JPY.tone: Haven Demand Surging — Carry Unwind, Hawkish BOJ Board Members Confirmed
JPY.summary: JPY is Bullish today as haven demand surges on the US-Iran escalation. The Russell 2000 entered correction territory (-10%), Asia-Pac stocks are opening sharply lower, and carry trades are unwinding. Two new hawkish BOJ board members — academics Toichiro Asada and Ayano Sato — have been confirmed, reinforcing the BOJ normalization narrative. PMT Asia-Pac Opening News highlights the risk-off mood as the primary driver. USD/JPY is under pressure. Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Haven demand surging: Russell 2000 in correction, Asia-Pac stocks sharply lower
Carry trades unwinding — JPY bid across the board
New hawkish BOJ board members confirmed: Asada and Sato (academics/reflationists)
US-Iran escalation: Trump 48-hour ultimatum, potential Kharg Island ground invasion
USD/JPY under pressure; BOJ normalization narrative reinforced

AUD.tone: Metals Selloff — Gold -3%, Silver -4%, Risk-Off Overwhelms RBA Hike Story
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today as the metals complex collapses: gold down over 3% (below $4,400/oz), silver leading declines at -4%, with the selloff exacerbated by a break below key technical levels. AUD is highly correlated to gold and risk sentiment, and the risk-off environment overwhelms the post-RBA hike story. Asia-Pac stocks are opening lower. China: PBoC Governor Pan defended China's trade surplus and pledged continued loose monetary policy — supportive for China but insufficient to offset the global risk-off. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Gold down 3%+ below $4,400/oz — break beneath support exacerbated selling
Silver down 4% — metals complex in broad selloff
Asia-Pac stocks opening sharply lower — risk-off overwhelms RBA hike story
PBoC Governor Pan: continued loose monetary policy — supportive but insufficient offset
AUD/USD under pressure; post-RBA hike gains at risk of full reversal

CAD.tone: Oil Surge — WTI $98.23, Brent $112.19, Iraq Force Majeure, CAD Outperforms
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today as oil surges on the Iraq force majeure declaration and the US-Iran escalation. WTI settled $98.23/bbl (+$2.68), Brent $112.19/bbl (+$3.54). Iraq declared force majeure on all oilfields developed by foreign oil companies — Basra production collapsed from 3.3mln to 900k bpd. The US FX Wrap explicitly noted 'CAD outperforms.' Saudi Arabia is moving closer to the US/Israeli side, opening the King Fahd Air Base. No major CAD events this week — pure petro-proxy tracking oil. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION ALIGNED ✅
drivers: WTI settled $98.23/bbl (+$2.68), Brent $112.19/bbl (+$3.54) — oil surging
Iraq force majeure: Basra production collapsed from 3.3mln to 900k bpd
US FX Wrap: 'CAD outperforms' — petro-currency benefiting from oil surge
Saudi Arabia opening King Fahd Air Base to Americans — GCC moving to US/Israel side
No major CAD events this week — pure oil proxy

CHF.tone: Haven Demand Overrides SNB Intervention — Extreme Risk-Off Environment
CHF.summary: CHF is Bullish today as extreme risk-off conditions override the SNB's intervention resistance from last week. When geopolitical risk reaches this level — Trump ultimatum, potential ground invasion, Iran missiles at Diego Garcia — haven demand for CHF overwhelms official pushback. EUR/CHF is likely falling as EUR faces energy shock pressure while CHF attracts defensive flows. The SNB's explicit intervention language from Thursday is now being tested. Weekly Weak Bearish vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Extreme risk-off: Trump ultimatum, potential Kharg Island invasion, Iran missiles at Diego Garcia
Haven demand overrides SNB intervention resistance — geopolitical risk too elevated
EUR/CHF likely falling: EUR faces energy shock, CHF attracts defensive flows
SNB's 'willingness to intervene' language now being tested by extreme risk-off
CHF bid alongside JPY as carry trades unwind globally

NZD.tone: High-Beta Risk Proxy — Metals Collapse, No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today as the high-beta risk proxy suffers from the metals collapse and global risk-off. Gold down 3%+, silver down 4%, Asia-Pac stocks lower — NZD has no domestic catalyst to offset the external pressure. RBNZ Governor Breman speaks tomorrow (02:00 FFT) — a soft tone would add further pressure. AUD/NZD is mixed as both Antipodeans are under pressure, but NZD is the weaker of the two given the absence of any rates support. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ALIGNED ✅
drivers: High-beta risk proxy: gold -3%, silver -4%, Asia-Pac stocks sharply lower
No domestic catalyst to offset global risk-off pressure
RBNZ Governor Breman speaks tomorrow (02:00 FFT) — soft tone would add pressure
AUD/NZD mixed but NZD weaker — no rates support vs AUD's RBA hike story
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed
